title: arctotis revoluta
intro: Arctotis revoluta, also known as the Cape marguerite, is a flowering plant native to the Western Cape region of South Africa, characterized by its bright yellow or orange daisy-like flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Arctotis revoluta include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herbal remedy. The plant can be prepared as a decoction or infusion by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, or as a tincture by extracting the active compounds with a solvent.
